content.problemStatement:
There are **three** friends and a total of NNN candies.

There will be a fight amongst the friends if all of them do **not** get the same number of candies.

Chef wants to divide **all** the candies such that there is **no fight**. Find whether such distribution is possible.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single integer NNN - the number of candies.


Output Format
For each test case, output `YES`, if we can distribute all the candies between the three friends equally. Otherwise output `NO`.


You can output each character of the answer in uppercase or lowercase. For example, the strings `yEs`, `yes`, `Yes`, and YES are considered the same.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N, the number of candies.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output "YES" if N can be divided equally among three friends, otherwise output "NO". Each answer on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: 3 is divisible by 3 → YES.
Test case 2: 4 is not divisible by 3 → NO.
Test case 3: 2 is not divisible by 3 → NO.
Test case 4: 6 is divisible by 3 → YES.
All three numbers (9, 12, 15) are multiples of 3, so each test case outputs YES.
Only N=3 is a multiple of 3; the others are not.

solution.approach: For each test case we only need to check whether N is a multiple of 3. If N % 3 == 0, the candies can be split equally among the three friends, otherwise they cannot.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
